next_steps: Skin biopsy of a representative rash lesion for histology and immunofluorescence
Serum testing for ANCA, ANA, ENA panel, C3/C4, and HIV/Syphilis serology
Biopsy of the nasal mucosal crusts/lesions
Urinalysis with microscopy to check for active sediment/hematuria
Chest CT scan to look for subtle parenchymal findings or airway involvement
Consultation with ENT for nasal endoscopy and biopsy
Serum RPR and treponemal-specific testing (e.g., FTA-ABS or TPPA)
Skin biopsy of a rash lesion for histopathology, immunofluorescence, and immunohistochemistry (treponemal staining)
Serum testing for ANA, ENA panel, ANCA, and serum ferritin
Urinalysis with microscopic examination to identify subclinical glomerular involvement
Peripheral blood smear examination for vacuolated myeloid precursors (suspicion for VEXAS)
ENT consultation for nasal endoscopy and biopsy of nasal crusting
